data.title: Starbucks adds energy-boosted refreshers to menu
data.description:
• Starbucks (SBUX) launched new energy refreshers in U.S. stores from April 7, boosting classic flavors with natural caffeine and B vitamins, the company said on Monday.
  • All refreshers can now also be made caffeine-free.

data.title: Toyota overtakes BYD in Japan EV sales with $6,000 subsidy advantage
data.description:
• Toyota (TM) has gained a strong edge over BYD (BYDDF) (BYDDY) in Japan's EV market, driven by favorable government subsidies and robust sales of its updated bZ4X SUV, according to a Nikkei report.
  • The government subsidies for its models increased by roughly $6,000 compared with the Chinese automaker, giving Toyota (TM) a significant pricing advantage for consumers.
  • Under Japan’s updated EV incentive program, Toyota (TM) models qualify for up to ¥1.3 million ($8,188), while BYD’s (BYDDF) vehicles remain eligible for only ¥350,000–¥450,000 ($2,204–$2,834). This gap of nearly $6,000 per vehicle significantly alters the cost equation for buyers.
  • BYD (BYDDF) had previously led Japan’s EV market in 2024, selling approximately 2,223 vehicles compared with Toyota’s (TM) 2,038.
  • Furthermore, Toyota (TM) sold over 2,000 bZ4X units in Japan in February 2026, its fourth straight month as the top domestic EV.

data.title: LG Electronics stock reverses gains despite record Q1 preliminary revenue and 33% profit surge
data.description:
LG Electronics, a subsidiary of the LG Group and the largest shareholder of LG Display (LPL), reported a sharp recovery in its first-quarter preliminary results on Tuesday, significantly outperforming market expectations, according to a WSJ report.

According to the Wall Street Journal, the South Korean tech giant swung back to a substantial profit after its first quarterly loss in nine years, driven by record sales in its core home appliance business and improved operational efficiencies.

The company expects a consolidated operating profit of 1.67 trillion won ($1.1 billion) for the January-March period, a 33% increase compared to last year, after an operating loss of 109 billion won in the previous quarter. Revenue is projected to reach a record 23.73 trillion won, attributed to cost improvements and growth in higher-margin businesses.

According to market estimates compiled by FnGuide, the consensus forecast stood at 23.32 trillion won in revenue and 1.38 trillion won in operating profit.

LG reported improved earnings due to early actions to reduce tariff risks, production optimization, cost structure enhancements, and growth in higher-margin sectors, the report said.

As per the report, the home appliance division contributed significantly, driven by strong competitiveness in both premium and mass markets, as well as growth in online sales and subscription services.

The vehicle solutions business experienced steady growth, supported by a solid order backlog and improved profitability, helped by favorable foreign exchange. 

Conversely, the HVAC division's revenue and profit declined due to geopolitical uncertainties in the Middle East. The company will focus on growth areas like heat pumps and cooling solutions for AI data centers.

The stock opened up 2.2% following the announcement; however, the shares had reversed and were trading at 107,600 won, representing a 1.7% drop from the previous day's close.

The company is scheduled to release its finalized, audited first-quarter financial statement later this month.

data.title: SpaceX details IPO schedule; retail demand expected to break records - report
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) provided details of its IPO during a Monday night meeting with its bankers, indicating that a significant portion of shares will be allocated to retail investors and that it plans to host 1,500 of them at a June event, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The Elon Musk-led company plans to kick off its IPO roadshow the week of June 8, where the company representatives and bankers will pitch to the investors. SpaceX (SPACE) has scheduled a meeting with around 125 financial analysts from the 21 banks involved in the deal the day before.

"Retail is going to be a critical part of this and ​a bigger part than any IPO in history," CFO Bret Johnsen said during the virtual meeting, according to the sources. He added, "Those are folks that have been incredibly supportive of us and of Elon (Musk) for a long time, and we want to make ​sure that we recognize that." 

One of SpaceX’s (SPACE) lead underwriters told the group of 21 investment banks that the level of retail demand and the size of the retail allocation would be something they have “never seen before.”

On June 11, SpaceX (SPACE) plans to host 1,500 retail investors at what the sources described as a major investor event. Alongside the U.S. investors, retail investors from the UK, EU, Australia, Canada, Japan, and Korea will also have the opportunity to participate in the offering, the report added. The IPO prospectus will be made ​public in late May. 

Further details like the structure of the deal and precise amount of the retail allocation are ​expected to be finalized closer to ​the IPO launch.

Previous reports indicated that founder Elon Musk aimed to allocate up to 30% of SpaceX’s (SPACE) shares to retail, far above the typical 5% to 10% seen in most IPOs. Recently, the space tech company (SPACE) raised its IPO target valuation to more than $2T.

Among the lead bookrunners for the IPO are Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C).

data.title: Tiger Global, Viking pummeled last month amid war in Iran: report
data.description:
Some of the hedge fund industry’s most prolific stock-pickers incurred big losses in March, as market turbulence driven by the Iran war rattled equities and upended trading strategies.

Tiger Global Management’s main hedge fund tumbled 7.3% last month, while Maverick Capital’s dropped 5% and Viking Global Investors’ lost 4.1%, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter. Coatue Management declined 4.8% in March, according to an earlier report. 

Last month Goldman Sachs (GS) reported a sharp acceleration in hedge fund selling activity, underscoring mounting pressure across U.S. equities as institutional sentiment continues to deteriorate.

According to the investment bank’s latest flow data covering March 20 through March 26, hedge funds were net sellers of U.S. stocks for a sixth consecutive week, with the pace of selling reaching its fastest level since April 2025. 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Samsung Electronics projects eightfold profit jump driven by AI chips
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) stock rose on the Korean exchange on Tuesday after the tech giant projected record‑breaking Q1 profit driven by surging AI demand.

Its preliminary guidance showed Q1 operating profit of about 57.2T Korean won, representing more than an eight‑fold increase from roughly 6.7T won a year ago and well above the LSEG estimate of around 40.6T won. Revenue for the quarter is projected to jump ~68% Y/Y to about 133T won, nearly triple its previous quarterly record.

The jump is largely attributed to pricing power and strong orders in Samsung’s memory‑chip business, especially high‑bandwidth memory used in AI accelerators and data‑center GPUs. The company has also benefited from ​a slump in the South Korean currency to a near 17-year low against the U.S. dollar.

Samsung (SSNLF) will provide a complete earnings report later this month.

data.title: Australia’s service sector hits 26-month low as PMI plunges into contraction amid inflation spike
data.description:
The S&P Global Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index fell to 46.3 in March 2026, larger than initial estimates of a 46.6 contraction, down from 52.8 in February, marking the first contraction in 26 months and the sharpest decline since November 2023.

The S&P Global Australia Composite PMI fell to 46.6 in March, worse than initial estimates of 47, down from 52.4 in February, marking the first contraction in private sector activity in a year and a half. 

Separate data showed Australia’s Monthly Inflation Gauge, compiled by the Melbourne Institute, climbed 1.3% month-on-month in March 2026, reversing a 0.2% drop in the previous month.

ANZ-Indeed Australian Job Ads slipped 3.1% month-on-month in March 2026, reversing February’s 16-month high of 3.2% and signaling the first decline in three months.

Household spending in Australia rose 0.3% month-on-month in February 2026, matching January’s pace and marking a second straight month of growth. 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index surged 173 points, or 2.0%, to a four-week top of 8,753 in early deals Tuesday. The Australian dollar remained weak around $0.690, staying near two-month lows as caution built ahead of US President Trump’s deadline regarding the Strait of Hormuz.

More on Australia:

data.title: Anthropic targets $30B revenue, signs TPU deal with Google and Broadcom
data.description:
Anthropic (ANTHRO) has struck a deal with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Broadcom (AVGO) for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity starting in 2027, as surging demand drives its annualized revenue toward $30 billion and prompts massive spending on chips and cloud infrastructure.

This significant expansion of compute infrastructure will power its frontier Claude models, Anthropic (ANTHRO) said in a statement.

The vast majority of the new compute will be sited in the United States, making the partnership a major expansion of its November 2025 commitment to invest $50 billion in strengthening American computing infrastructure.

Around 3.5 GW of capacity on Google’s hardware will come through a partnership with chipmaker Broadcom, starting from next year, according to a separate filing on Monday. In all, the deal would give Anthropic access to close to 5 GW in new computing capacity over the coming years, according to a person with knowledge of the terms.

Anthropic (ANTHRO) said demand from Claude customers has accelerated in 2026. Its run-rate revenue has now surpassed $30 billion—up from approximately $9 billion at the end of 2025.

“We are building the capacity necessary to serve the exponential growth we have seen in our customer base while also enabling Claude to define the frontier of AI development,” said Krishna Rao, Anthropic’s chief financial officer. Broadcom shares rose almost 3% after the market closed on Monday. The company also announced that it would develop and supply custom TPUs for Google as part of a long-term agreement through 2031.

Anthropic’s rival OpenAI (OPENAI) last year struck a string of computing deals with Broadcom, Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and others in a push to lock in as much capacity as possible to power its AI tools.

data.title: Entergy declares $0.64 dividend
data.description:
• Entergy (ETR) declares $0.64/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.23%
  • Payable June 1; for shareholders of record May 1; ex-div May 1.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.64 for three consecutive quarters.
  • See ETR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Apple foldable iPhone faces engineering setbacks, potential production delay: Nikkei
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) is seeing setbacks in the engineering test phase of the foldable iPhone and could push back its production and shipment schedule, Nikkei Asia reported, citing unidentified people.
  • Engineering issues are more complex and taking more time to resolve than expected. The first shipment could be delayed by months, the report added.
